justification: All 4 business_impact sub-fields (financial, operational, user, risk) are substantively populated with multi-sentence analysis. 7 distinct stakeholder groups identified with specific impact per group. 4 escalations for missing information. Contractual SLA risk explicitly identified in both risk field and escalations. Confidence level is 52 (well under 80) with detailed assumptions listed. Competitive positioning explicitly mentioned in financial and risk sections.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough stakeholder breakdown with 7 groups including premium customers, B2B, support, ops, partners, and product/marketing
Confidence level of 52 is well-calibrated given missing contract and tier scope information
Competitive risk explicitly calls out Amazon/FedEx/UPS trend toward more precise windows
4 distinct escalations covering tier scope, SLA measurement vs display, API contracts, and business motivation
Correctly identifies that SLA measurement change must not happen implicitly as side effect of UI change
Business rules include premium tier exclusion (BR-5) and internal precision retention (BR-3)

trials.justification: All 4 business_impact sub-fields (financial, operational, user, risk) are substantively populated with multi-sentence analysis. 6 distinct stakeholder groups identified with specific impact per group (end customers, customer support, operations/dispatch, B2B/marketplace partners, product/commercial teams, finance/SLA management). 3 escalations raised for missing information (service tier scope, cutoff time definition, business motivation). observations.text: Exceptionally thorough business impact analysis across all four dimensions with balanced positive/negative framing
Six stakeholder groups identified, exceeding the minimum of 3, each with specific and actionable impact descriptions
Confidence level of 52 is well-calibrated given missing contract and tier information
Three substantive escalations with options and recommendations, not just flags
Contractual SLA risk clearly identified for B2B/marketplace partners
Competitive positioning explicitly addressed with reference to Amazon/DoorDash setting market expectations
Identified the measurement risk of inflated on-time metrics masking real problems — a subtle but important insight
Operational benefit is balanced against customer experience cost, not presented one-sidedly

trials.justification: All 6 pass criteria met: (1) All 4 business_impact sub-fields (financial, operational, user, risk) contain substantive multi-sentence analysis. (2) stakeholders_affected lists 7 distinct groups with specific impact per group. (3) Three escalations for missing context (motivation, tier scope, date definition). (4) Contractual SLA risk explicitly identified in risk field and B2B escalation. (5) confidence.level=50, well within <=80, with detailed assumptions. (6) Competitive positioning explicitly mentioned in risk field referencing Amazon/FedEx/UPS trend.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough business_impact across all 4 dimensions with specific, actionable analysis rather than generic statements
7 stakeholder groups identified (far exceeding the 3 minimum), each with specific impact descriptions
3 well-structured escalations with options and recommendations — goes beyond minimum requirement
Contractual SLA risk called out in risk field, B2B edge case, acceptance criteria AC-6, and dedicated escalation
Confidence level of 50 is well-calibrated given missing contract and motivation information
Competitive positioning explicitly addresses industry trend toward MORE precise ETAs
Added rejection_reasons identifying hard invariant violations — shows domain authority
Edge cases include timezone handling and midnight-spanning deliveries — nuanced domain thinking
